authors_text: Arul Selvam Periyasamy,Catherine Capellen,Max Schwarz,Sven Behnke
title: ConvPoseCNN2: Prediction and Refinement of Dense 6D Object Poses
title_zh: ConvPoseCNN2：密集 6D 物体姿势的预测和细化
comment: ef:Communications in Computer and Information Science (CCIS), vol. 1474, pp. 353-371, Springer, 2022
comments: ef:Communications in Computer and Information Science (CCIS), vol. 1474, pp. 353-371, Springer, 2022
abstract: Object pose estimation is a key perceptual capability in robotics. We propose a fully-convolutional extension of the PoseCNN method, which densely predicts object translations and orientations. This has several advantages such as improving the spatial resolution of the orientation predictions -- useful in highly-cluttered arrangements, significant reduction in parameters by avoiding full connectivity, and fast inference. We propose and discuss several aggregation methods for dense orientation predictions that can be applied as a post-processing step, such as averaging and clustering techniques. We demonstrate that our method achieves the same accuracy as PoseCNN on the challenging YCB-Video dataset and provide a detailed ablation study of several variants of our method. Finally, we demonstrate that the model can be further improved by inserting an iterative refinement module into the middle of the network, which enforces consistency of the prediction.
summary: Object pose estimation is a key perceptual capability in robotics. We propose a fully-convolutional extension of the PoseCNN method, which densely predicts object translations and orientations. This has several advantages such as improving the spatial resolution of the orientation predictions -- useful in highly-cluttered arrangements, significant reduction in parameters by avoiding full connectivity, and fast inference. We propose and discuss several aggregation methods for dense orientation predictions that can be applied as a post-processing step, such as averaging and clustering techniques. We demonstrate that our method achieves the same accuracy as PoseCNN on the challenging YCB-Video dataset and provide a detailed ablation study of several variants of our method. Finally, we demonstrate that the model can be further improved by inserting an iterative refinement module into the middle of the network, which enforces consistency of the prediction.
abstract_zh: 物体姿态估计是机器人技术中的关键感知能力。我们提出了 PoseCNN 方法的全卷积扩展，该方法可以密集地预测对象平移和方向。这有几个优点，例如提高方向预测的空间分辨率——在高度混乱的排列中很有用，通过避免完全连接来显着减少参数，以及快速推理。我们提出并讨论了几种用于密集方向预测的聚合方法，这些方法可以用作后处理步骤，例如平均和聚类技术。我们证明我们的方法在具有挑战性的 YCB-Video 数据集上达到了与 PoseCNN 相同的精度，并提供了我们方法的几种变体的详细消融研究。最后，我们证明可以通过在网络中间插入迭代细化模块来进一步改进模型，从而增强预测的一致性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ConvPoseCNN2方法，通过全卷积网络实现密集6D物体姿态预测与优化。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在复杂场景下存在空间分辨率不足、参数冗余及推理速度慢等问题，需改进姿态估计效率与准确性。
AI.method: 设计全卷积网络结构实现密集预测，采用平均与聚类等聚合方法进行后处理，并引入迭代优化模块增强预测一致性。
AI.result: 在YCB-Video数据集上达到与PoseCNN相当的精度，消融实验验证方法有效性，迭代模块进一步提升性能。
AI.conclusion: ConvPoseCNN2在保持精度的同时优化了计算效率，迭代机制有效提升预测一致性，为密集姿态估计提供新思路。

We propose and discuss several aggregation methods for dense orientation predictions that can be applied as a post-processing step, such as averaging and clustering techniques. We demonstrate that our method achieves the same accuracy as PoseCNN on the challenging YCB-Video dataset and provide a detailed ablation study of several variants of our method. Finally, we demonstrate that the model can be further improved by inserting an iterative refinement module into the middle of the network, which enforces consistency of the prediction. 物体姿态估计是机器人技术中的关键感知能力。我们提出了 PoseCNN 方法的全卷积扩展，该方法可以密集地预测对象平移和方向。这有几个优点，例如提高方向预测的空间分辨率——在高度混乱的排列中很有用，通过避免完全连接来显着减少参数，以及快速推理。我们提出并讨论了几种用于密集方向预测的聚合方法，这些方法可以用作后处理步骤，例如平均和聚类技术。我们证明我们的方法在具有挑战性的 YCB-Video 数据集上达到了与 PoseCNN 相同的精度，并提供了我们方法的几种变体的详细消融研究。最后，我们证明可以通过在网络中间插入迭代细化模块来进一步改进模型，从而增强预测的一致性。 本文提出ConvPoseCNN2方法，通过全卷积网络实现密集6D物体姿态预测与优化。 Arul Selvam Periyasamy,Catherine Capellen,Max Schwarz,Sven Behnke cs.CV

authors_text: Paul Scherer,Alison Pouplin,Alice Del Vecchio,Suraj M S,Oliver Bolton,Jyothish Soman,Jake P. Taylor-King,Lindsay Edwards,Thomas Gaudelet
title: PyRelationAL: a python library for active learning research and development
title_zh: PyRelationAL：用于主动学习研究和开发的 python 库
abstract: Active learning (AL) is a sub-field of ML focused on the development of methods to iteratively and economically acquire data by strategically querying new data points that are the most useful for a particular task. Here, we introduce PyRelationAL, an open source library for AL research. We describe a modular toolkit based around a two step design methodology for composing pool-based active learning strategies applicable to both single-acquisition and batch-acquisition strategies. This framework allows for the mathematical and practical specification of a broad number of existing and novel strategies under a consistent programming model and abstraction. Furthermore, we incorporate datasets and active learning tasks applicable to them to simplify comparative evaluation and benchmarking, along with an initial group of benchmarks across datasets included in this library. The toolkit is compatible with existing ML frameworks. PyRelationAL is maintained using modern software engineering practices -- with an inclusive contributor code of conduct -- to promote long term library quality and utilisation. PyRelationAL is available under a permissive Apache licence on PyPi and at https://github.com/RelationRx/pyrelational.
summary: Active learning (AL) is a sub-field of ML focused on the development of methods to iteratively and economically acquire data by strategically querying new data points that are the most useful for a particular task. Here, we introduce PyRelationAL, an open source library for AL research. We describe a modular toolkit based around a two step design methodology for composing pool-based active learning strategies applicable to both single-acquisition and batch-acquisition strategies. This framework allows for the mathematical and practical specification of a broad number of existing and novel strategies under a consistent programming model and abstraction. Furthermore, we incorporate datasets and active learning tasks applicable to them to simplify comparative evaluation and benchmarking, along with an initial group of benchmarks across datasets included in this library. The toolkit is compatible with existing ML frameworks. PyRelationAL is maintained using modern software engineering practices -- with an inclusive contributor code of conduct -- to promote long term library quality and utilisation. PyRelationAL is available under a permissive Apache licence on PyPi and at https://github.com/RelationRx/pyrelational.
abstract_zh: 主动学习 (AL) 是 ML 的一个子领域，专注于开发通过战略性查询对特定任务最有用的新数据点来迭代且经济地获取数据的方法。在这里，我们介绍 PyRelationAL，一个用于 AL 研究的开源库。我们描述了一个基于两步设计方法的模块化工具包，用于构建适用于单次采集和批量采集策略的基于池的主动学习策略。该框架允许在一致的编程模型和抽象下对大量现有和新颖的策略进行数学和实践规范。此外，我们合并了适用于它们的数据集和主动学习任务，以简化比较评估和基准测试，以及该库中包含的跨数据集的初始基准组。该工具包与现有的机器学习框架兼容。 PyRelationAL 使用现代软件工程实践（具有包容性的贡献者行为准则）进行维护，以提高库的长期质量和利用率。 PyRelationAL 可在 PyPi 上的许可 Apache 许可下使用，网址为 https://github.com/RelationRx/pyrelational。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个名为PyRelationAL的Python库，旨在为基于池的主动学习策略提供模块化工具和基准测试框架。
AI.motivation: 主动学习研究需要灵活实现和比较多种策略，但现有工具缺乏统一接口和标准化基准，限制了方法开发与评估效率。
AI.method: 提出两步设计方法构建工具包，支持单次/批量获取策略的数学与工程实现，集成标准数据集及基准任务，并兼容主流机器学习框架。
AI.result: 实现了多种经典与新型主动学习策略，提供跨数据集的基准测试套件，通过现代软件工程实践维护，以Apache许可证开源发布。
AI.conclusion: PyRelationAL为研究者提供了标准化的主动学习实验平台，推动了策略创新与性能比较，有助于促进该领域的长期发展。

authors_text: Manxi Lin,Zahra Bashir,Martin Grønnebæk Tolsgaard,Anders Nymark Christensen,Aasa Feragen
title: DTU-Net: Learning Topological Similarity for Curvilinear Structure Segmentation
title_zh: DTU-Net：学习曲线结构分割的拓扑相似性
abstract: Curvilinear structure segmentation is important in medical imaging, quantifying structures such as vessels, airways, neurons, or organ boundaries in 2D slices. Segmentation via pixel-wise classification often fails to capture the small and low-contrast curvilinear structures. Prior topological information is typically used to address this problem, often at an expensive computational cost, and sometimes requiring prior knowledge of the expected topology. We present DTU-Net, a data-driven approach to topology-preserving curvilinear structure segmentation. DTU-Net consists of two sequential, lightweight U-Nets, dedicated to texture and topology, respectively. While the texture net makes a coarse prediction using image texture information, the topology net learns topological information from the coarse prediction by employing a triplet loss trained to recognize false and missed splits in the structure. We conduct experiments on a challenging multi-class ultrasound scan segmentation dataset as well as a well-known retinal imaging dataset. Results show that our model outperforms existing approaches in both pixel-wise segmentation accuracy and topological continuity, with no need for prior topological knowledge.
summary: Curvilinear structure segmentation is important in medical imaging, quantifying structures such as vessels, airways, neurons, or organ boundaries in 2D slices. Segmentation via pixel-wise classification often fails to capture the small and low-contrast curvilinear structures. Prior topological information is typically used to address this problem, often at an expensive computational cost, and sometimes requiring prior knowledge of the expected topology. We present DTU-Net, a data-driven approach to topology-preserving curvilinear structure segmentation. DTU-Net consists of two sequential, lightweight U-Nets, dedicated to texture and topology, respectively. While the texture net makes a coarse prediction using image texture information, the topology net learns topological information from the coarse prediction by employing a triplet loss trained to recognize false and missed splits in the structure. We conduct experiments on a challenging multi-class ultrasound scan segmentation dataset as well as a well-known retinal imaging dataset. Results show that our model outperforms existing approaches in both pixel-wise segmentation accuracy and topological continuity, with no need for prior topological knowledge.
abstract_zh: 曲线结构分割在医学成像中非常重要，可以量化二维切片中的血管、气道、神经元或器官边界等结构。通过像素级分类进行的分割通常无法捕获小且低对比度的曲线结构。先验拓扑信息通常用于解决此问题，通常需要昂贵的计算成本，并且有时需要预期拓扑的先验知识。我们提出了 DTU-Net，一种数据驱动的拓扑保持曲线结构分割方法。 DTU-Net 由两个连续的轻量级 U-Net 组成，分别专用于纹理和拓扑。虽然纹理网络使用图像纹理信息进行粗略预测，但拓扑网络通过使用经过训练来识别结构中的错误和遗漏分割的三元组损失来从粗略预测中学习拓扑信息。我们在具有挑战性的多类超声扫描分割数据集以及众所周知的视网膜成像数据集上进行了实验。结果表明，我们的模型在像素分割精度和拓扑连续性方面都优于现有方法，并且不需要先验拓扑知识。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DTU-Net的深度学习模型，用于医学图像中曲线条结构的分割，该模型能够保留拓扑结构信息。
AI.motivation: 传统的像素级分类方法在分割小而对比度低的曲线条结构时效果不佳，而利用先验拓扑信息的方法计算成本高且需要先验知识。
AI.method: DTU-Net由两个轻量级的U-Net组成，分别用于纹理和拓扑结构的学习。纹理网络进行粗略预测，拓扑网络通过三元组损失学习拓扑信息，识别结构中的错误和遗漏。
AI.result: 在多类超声扫描分割数据集和视网膜成像数据集上的实验表明，该模型在像素级分割精度和拓扑连续性方面优于现有方法，且无需先验拓扑知识。
AI.conclusion: DTU-Net提供了一种无需先验知识的数据驱动方法，能够有效分割曲线条结构，并在多个数据集上取得了优异的性能。

authors_text: Ning Gao,Jingyu Zhang,Ruijie Chen,Ngo Anh Vien,Hanna Ziesche,Gerhard Neumann
title: Meta-Learning Regrasping Strategies for Physical-Agnostic Objects
title_zh: 物理不可知对象的元学习重新抓取策略
comment: Accepted as spotlight in ICRA 2022 Workshop: Scaling Robot Learning
comments: Accepted as spotlight in ICRA 2022 Workshop: Scaling Robot Learning
abstract: Grasping inhomogeneous objects in real-world applications remains a challenging task due to the unknown physical properties such as mass distribution and coefficient of friction. In this study, we propose a meta-learning algorithm called ConDex, which incorporates Conditional Neural Processes (CNP) with DexNet-2.0 to autonomously discern the underlying physical properties of objects using depth images. ConDex efficiently acquires physical embeddings from limited trials, enabling precise grasping point estimation. Furthermore, ConDex is capable of updating the predicted grasping quality iteratively from new trials in an online fashion. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first who generate two object datasets focusing on inhomogeneous physical properties with varying mass distributions and friction coefficients. Extensive evaluations in simulation demonstrate ConDex's superior performance over DexNet-2.0 and existing meta-learning-based grasping pipelines. Furthermore, ConDex shows robust generalization to previously unseen real-world objects despite training solely in the simulation. The synthetic and real-world datasets will be published as well.
summary: Grasping inhomogeneous objects in real-world applications remains a challenging task due to the unknown physical properties such as mass distribution and coefficient of friction. In this study, we propose a meta-learning algorithm called ConDex, which incorporates Conditional Neural Processes (CNP) with DexNet-2.0 to autonomously discern the underlying physical properties of objects using depth images. ConDex efficiently acquires physical embeddings from limited trials, enabling precise grasping point estimation. Furthermore, ConDex is capable of updating the predicted grasping quality iteratively from new trials in an online fashion. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first who generate two object datasets focusing on inhomogeneous physical properties with varying mass distributions and friction coefficients. Extensive evaluations in simulation demonstrate ConDex's superior performance over DexNet-2.0 and existing meta-learning-based grasping pipelines. Furthermore, ConDex shows robust generalization to previously unseen real-world objects despite training solely in the simulation. The synthetic and real-world datasets will be published as well.
abstract_zh: 由于质量分布和摩擦系数等未知的物理特性，在现实应用中抓取不均匀的物体仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。在本研究中，我们提出了一种名为 ConDex 的元学习算法，该算法将条件神经过程 (CNP) 与 DexNet-2.0 结合起来，使用深度图像自动识别物体的潜在物理属性。 ConDex 从有限的试验中有效地获取物理嵌入，从而实现精确的抓取点估计。此外，ConDex 能够通过在线方式迭代更新新试验的预测抓取质量。据我们所知，我们是第一个生成两个对象数据集的人，该数据集专注于具有不同质量分布和摩擦系数的不均匀物理特性。模拟中的广泛评估表明 ConDex 比 DexNet-2.0 和现有的基于元学习的抓取管道具有优越的性能。此外，尽管仅在模拟中进行训练，ConDex 对以前未见过的现实世界对象表现出了强大的泛化能力。合成数据集和真实数据集也将发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于元学习的算法ConDex，用于解决未知物理属性物体的自主抓取问题。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中物体的质量分布和摩擦系数等物理属性未知，导致传统抓取方法难以适应，需开发能自主学习物理特性的智能算法。
AI.method: 结合条件神经过程（CNP）与DexNet-2.0，通过深度图像学习物体物理嵌入表示，并在线迭代更新抓取质量预测，构建两个异质物理属性数据集。
AI.result: 仿真测试显示ConDex优于DexNet-2.0及现有元学习方法，且能泛化到未见过的真实物体，相关数据集将公开发布。
AI.conclusion: ConDex通过少量试错实现物理属性学习与抓取策略优化，为未知物体抓取提供了高效且通用的解决方案。

search_text: Meta-Learning Regrasping Strategies for Physical-Agnostic Objects 物理不可知对象的元学习重新抓取策略 Grasping inhomogeneous objects in real-world applications remains a challenging task due to the unknown physical properties such as mass distribution and coefficient of friction. In this study, we propose a meta-learning algorithm called ConDex, which incorporates Conditional Neural Processes (CNP) with DexNet-2.0 to autonomously discern the underlying physical properties of objects using depth images. ConDex efficiently acquires physical embeddings from limited trials, enabling precise grasping point estimation. Furthermore, ConDex is capable of updating the predicted grasping quality iteratively from new trials in an online fashion. To the best of our knowledge, we are the first who generate two object datasets focusing on inhomogeneous physical properties with varying mass distributions and friction coefficients. Extensive evaluations in simulation demonstrate ConDex's superior performance over DexNet-2.0 and existing meta-learning-based grasping pipelines. Furthermore, ConDex shows robust generalization to previously unseen real-world objects despite training solely in the simulation. The synthetic and real-world datasets will be published as well. 由于质量分布和摩擦系数等未知的物理特性，在现实应用中抓取不均匀的物体仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。在本研究中，我们提出了一种名为 ConDex 的元学习算法，该算法将条件神经过程 (CNP) 与 DexNet-2.0 结合起来，使用深度图像自动识别物体的潜在物理属性。 ConDex 从有限的试验中有效地获取物理嵌入，从而实现精确的抓取点估计。此外，ConDex 能够通过在线方式迭代更新新试验的预测抓取质量。据我们所知，我们是第一个生成两个对象数据集的人，该数据集专注于具有不同质量分布和摩擦系数的不均匀物理特性。模拟中的广泛评估表明 ConDex 比 DexNet-2.0 和现有的基于元学习的抓取管道具有优越的性能。此外，尽管仅在模拟中进行训练，ConDex 对以前未见过的现实世界对象表现出了强大的泛化能力。合成数据集和真实数据集也将发布。 本文提出一种基于元学习的算法ConDex，用于解决未知物理属性物体的自主抓取问题。 Ning Gao,Jingyu Zhang,Ruijie Chen,Ngo Anh Vien,Hanna Ziesche,Gerhard Neumann cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

title: Illuminating Salient Contributions in Neuron Activation with Attribution Equilibrium
title_zh: 用归因平衡阐明神经元激活的显着贡献
abstract: With the remarkable success of deep neural networks, there is a growing interest in research aimed at providing clear interpretations of their decision-making processes. In this paper, we introduce Attribution Equilibrium, a novel method to decompose output predictions into fine-grained attributions, balancing positive and negative relevance for clearer visualization of the evidence behind a network decision. We carefully analyze conventional approaches to decision explanation and present a different perspective on the conservation of evidence. We define the evidence as a gap between positive and negative influences among gradient-derived initial contribution maps. Then, we incorporate antagonistic elements and a user-defined criterion for the degree of positive attribution during propagation. Additionally, we consider the role of inactivated neurons in the propagation rule, thereby enhancing the discernment of less relevant elements such as the background. We conduct various assessments in a verified experimental environment with PASCAL VOC 2007, MS COCO 2014, and ImageNet datasets. The results demonstrate that our method outperforms existing attribution methods both qualitatively and quantitatively in identifying the key input features that influence model decisions.
summary: With the remarkable success of deep neural networks, there is a growing interest in research aimed at providing clear interpretations of their decision-making processes. In this paper, we introduce Attribution Equilibrium, a novel method to decompose output predictions into fine-grained attributions, balancing positive and negative relevance for clearer visualization of the evidence behind a network decision. We carefully analyze conventional approaches to decision explanation and present a different perspective on the conservation of evidence. We define the evidence as a gap between positive and negative influences among gradient-derived initial contribution maps. Then, we incorporate antagonistic elements and a user-defined criterion for the degree of positive attribution during propagation. Additionally, we consider the role of inactivated neurons in the propagation rule, thereby enhancing the discernment of less relevant elements such as the background. We conduct various assessments in a verified experimental environment with PASCAL VOC 2007, MS COCO 2014, and ImageNet datasets. The results demonstrate that our method outperforms existing attribution methods both qualitatively and quantitatively in identifying the key input features that influence model decisions.
abstract_zh: 随着深度神经网络取得的巨大成功，人们对旨在为其决策过程提供清晰解释的研究越来越感兴趣。在本文中，我们介绍了归因均衡，这是一种将输出预测分解为细粒度归因的新颖方法，平衡正相关性和负相关性，以便更清晰地可视化网络决策背后的证据。我们仔细分析了传统的决策解释方法，并对证据保护提出了不同的观点。我们将证据定义为梯度导出的初始贡献图之间的积极影响和消极影响之间的差距。然后，我们结合对抗性元素和用户定义的传播过程中积极归因程度的标准。此外，我们还考虑了失活神经元在传播规则中的作用，从而增强了对背景等不太相关元素的辨别能力。我们在经过验证的实验环境中使用 PASCAL VOC 2007、MS COCO 2014 和 ImageNet 数据集进行各种评估。结果表明，我们的方法在识别影响模型决策的关键输入特征方面在定性和定量方面都优于现有的归因方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Attribution Equilibrium的新方法，用于分解深度神经网络的输出预测，通过平衡正负相关性以更清晰地可视化决策依据。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在解释神经网络决策时难以平衡正负相关性，且对背景等不相关元素的区分能力不足，需改进归因方法以提升可视化清晰度。
AI.method: 定义证据为梯度初始贡献图中正负影响的差距，引入对抗元素和用户自定义正归因标准，并在传播规则中考虑非激活神经元的作用。
AI.result: 在PASCAL VOC 2007、MS COCO 2014和ImageNet数据集上，该方法在定性和定量指标上均优于现有归因方法。
AI.conclusion: Attribution Equilibrium通过平衡正负归因及增强背景辨别能力，有效提升了神经网络决策解释的准确性与清晰度。

authors_text: Petchiammal A,Briskline Kiruba S,D. Murugan,Pandarasamy A
title: Paddy Doctor: A Visual Image Dataset for Automated Paddy Disease Classification and Benchmarking
title_zh: Paddy Doctor：用于自动水稻病害分类和基准测试的视觉图像数据集
abstract: One of the critical biotic stress factors paddy farmers face is diseases caused by bacteria, fungi, and other organisms. These diseases affect plants' health severely and lead to significant crop loss. Most of these diseases can be identified by regularly observing the leaves and stems under expert supervision. In a country with vast agricultural regions and limited crop protection experts, manual identification of paddy diseases is challenging. Thus, to add a solution to this problem, it is necessary to automate the disease identification process and provide easily accessible decision support tools to enable effective crop protection measures. However, the lack of availability of public datasets with detailed disease information limits the practical implementation of accurate disease detection systems. This paper presents \emph{Paddy Doctor}, a visual image dataset for identifying paddy diseases. Our dataset contains 16,225 annotated paddy leaf images across 13 classes (12 diseases and normal leaf). We benchmarked the \emph{Paddy Doctor} dataset using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and four transfer learning based models (VGG16, MobileNet, Xception, and ResNet34). The experimental results showed that ResNet34 achieved the highest F1-score of 97.50%. We release our dataset and reproducible code in the open source for community use.
summary: One of the critical biotic stress factors paddy farmers face is diseases caused by bacteria, fungi, and other organisms. These diseases affect plants' health severely and lead to significant crop loss. Most of these diseases can be identified by regularly observing the leaves and stems under expert supervision. In a country with vast agricultural regions and limited crop protection experts, manual identification of paddy diseases is challenging. Thus, to add a solution to this problem, it is necessary to automate the disease identification process and provide easily accessible decision support tools to enable effective crop protection measures. However, the lack of availability of public datasets with detailed disease information limits the practical implementation of accurate disease detection systems. This paper presents \emph{Paddy Doctor}, a visual image dataset for identifying paddy diseases. Our dataset contains 16,225 annotated paddy leaf images across 13 classes (12 diseases and normal leaf). We benchmarked the \emph{Paddy Doctor} dataset using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and four transfer learning based models (VGG16, MobileNet, Xception, and ResNet34). The experimental results showed that ResNet34 achieved the highest F1-score of 97.50%. We release our dataset and reproducible code in the open source for community use.
abstract_zh: 稻农面临的关键生物胁迫因素之一是由细菌、真菌和其他生物引起的疾病。这些疾病严重影响植物的健康并导致农作物严重损失。大多数这些疾病可以通过在专家监督下定期观察叶子和茎来识别。在一个农业地区广阔且作物保护专家有限的国家，人工识别水稻病害具有挑战性。因此，为了解决这个问题，有必要实现疾病识别过程的自动化，并提供易于访问的决策支持工具，以实现有效的作物保护措施。然而，缺乏包含详细疾病信息的公共数据集限制了准确疾病检测系统的实际实施。本文提出了 \emph{Paddy Doctor}，一个用于识别水稻病害的视觉图像数据集。我们的数据集包含 13 个类别（12 种疾病和正常叶子）的 16,225 个带注释的稻叶图像。我们使用卷积神经网络 (CNN) 和四个基于迁移学习的模型（VGG16、MobileNet、Xception 和 ResNet34）对 \emph{Paddy Doctor} 数据集进行了基准测试。实验结果表明，ResNet34取得了最高的F1分数，达到97.50%。我们以开源方式发布数据集和可重现的代码以供社区使用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为Paddy Doctor的水稻病害图像数据集，用于自动化水稻病害分类与基准测试。
AI.motivation: 水稻种植中病害检测依赖专家人工识别，但农业区域广阔且专家资源有限，导致作物损失严重。现有公开数据集不足阻碍了精准检测系统的开发。
AI.method: 构建包含16,225张标注水稻叶片图像的13类数据集（12种病害+健康叶片），采用CNN及VGG16、MobileNet、Xception、ResNet34等迁移学习模型进行基准测试。
AI.result: ResNet34模型在数据集上达到最高F1分数97.50%，数据集和代码已开源共享。
AI.conclusion: Paddy Doctor数据集为水稻病害检测研究提供基准，有助于推动自动化病害识别技术的发展与应用。

ai_content_json.result: ResNet34模型在数据集上达到最高F1分数97.50%，数据集和代码已开源共享。
ai_content_json.conclusion: Paddy Doctor数据集为水稻病害检测研究提供基准，有助于推动自动化病害识别技术的发展与应用。

search_text: Paddy Doctor: A Visual Image Dataset for Automated Paddy Disease Classification and Benchmarking Paddy Doctor：用于自动水稻病害分类和基准测试的视觉图像数据集 One of the critical biotic stress factors paddy farmers face is diseases caused by bacteria, fungi, and other organisms. These diseases affect plants' health severely and lead to significant crop loss. Most of these diseases can be identified by regularly observing the leaves and stems under expert supervision. In a country with vast agricultural regions and limited crop protection experts, manual identification of paddy diseases is challenging. Thus, to add a solution to this problem, it is necessary to automate the disease identification process and provide easily accessible decision support tools to enable effective crop protection measures. However, the lack of availability of public datasets with detailed disease information limits the practical implementation of accurate disease detection systems. This paper presents \emph{Paddy Doctor}, a visual image dataset for identifying paddy diseases. Our dataset contains 16,225 annotated paddy leaf images across 13 classes (12 diseases and normal leaf). We benchmarked the \emph{Paddy Doctor} dataset using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and four transfer learning based models (VGG16, MobileNet, Xception, and ResNet34). The experimental results showed that ResNet34 achieved the highest F1-score of 97.50%. We release our dataset and reproducible code in the open source for community use. 稻农面临的关键生物胁迫因素之一是由细菌、真菌和其他生物引起的疾病。这些疾病严重影响植物的健康并导致农作物严重损失。大多数这些疾病可以通过在专家监督下定期观察叶子和茎来识别。在一个农业地区广阔且作物保护专家有限的国家，人工识别水稻病害具有挑战性。因此，为了解决这个问题，有必要实现疾病识别过程的自动化，并提供易于访问的决策支持工具，以实现有效的作物保护措施。然而，缺乏包含详细疾病信息的公共数据集限制了准确疾病检测系统的实际实施。本文提出了 \emph{Paddy Doctor}，一个用于识别水稻病害的视觉图像数据集。我们的数据集包含 13 个类别（12 种疾病和正常叶子）的 16,225 个带注释的稻叶图像。我们使用卷积神经网络 (CNN) 和四个基于迁移学习的模型（VGG16、MobileNet、Xception 和 ResNet34）对 \emph{Paddy Doctor} 数据集进行了基准测试。实验结果表明，ResNet34取得了最高的F1分数，达到97.50%。我们以开源方式发布数据集和可重现的代码以供社区使用。 本文提出一个名为Paddy Doctor的水稻病害图像数据集，用于自动化水稻病害分类与基准测试。 Petchiammal A,Briskline Kiruba S,D. Murugan,Pandarasamy A cs.CV

title: Proceedings Seventeenth International Workshop on the ACL2 Theorem Prover and its Applications
title_zh: 第十七届 ACL2 定理证明器及其应用国际研讨会论文集
abstract: This volume contains a selection of papers presented at the 17th International Workshop on the ACL2 Theorem Prover and its Applications (ACL2 2022). The workshops are the premier technical forum for presenting research and experiences related to ACL2.
summary: This volume contains a selection of papers presented at the 17th International Workshop on the ACL2 Theorem Prover and its Applications (ACL2 2022). The workshops are the premier technical forum for presenting research and experiences related to ACL2.
abstract_zh: 本卷包含在第 17 届 ACL2 定理证明器及其应用国际研讨会 (ACL2 2022) 上发表的论文选集。这些研讨会是展示 ACL2 相关研究和经验的首要技术论坛。

AI.tldr: 本文集收录了第17届ACL2定理证明器及其应用国际研讨会的论文，旨在展示与ACL2相关的研究和应用经验。
AI.motivation: 为ACL2定理证明器的研究者和实践者提供一个交流平台，促进该领域的技术进步与应用推广。
AI.method: 通过筛选和收录参会者提交的论文，集中展示ACL2在形式化验证、软件工程等领域的最新研究成果与应用案例。
AI.result: 形成了包含多篇精选论文的会议文集，反映了ACL2社区的最新研究动态与技术实践。
AI.conclusion: 该研讨会巩固了ACL2作为形式化方法重要工具的地位，推动了其在学术界和工业界的应用深化。

authors_text: Jannik Castenow,Björn Feldkord,Till Knollmann,Manuel Malatyali,Friedhelm Meyer auf der Heide
title: The k-Server with Preferences Problem
title_zh: 具有首选项问题的 k-Server
comment: A conference version of this paper was accepted at the 34th ACM Symposium on Parallelism in Algorithms and Architectures (SPAA 2022)
comments: A conference version of this paper was accepted at the 34th ACM Symposium on Parallelism in Algorithms and Architectures (SPAA 2022)
abstract: The $k$-Server Problem covers plenty of resource allocation scenarios, and several variations have been studied extensively for decades. We present a model generalizing the $k$-Server Problem by preferences of the requests, where the servers are not identical and requests can express which specific servers should serve them. In our model, requests can either be answered by any server (general requests) or by a specific one (specific requests). If only general requests appear, the instance is one of the original $k$-Server Problem, and a lower bound for the competitive ratio of $k$ applies. If only specific requests appear, a solution with a competitive ratio of $1$ becomes trivial. We show that if both kinds of requests appear, the lower bound raises to $2k-1$. We study deterministic online algorithms and present two algorithms for uniform metrics. The first one has a competitive ratio dependent on the frequency of specific requests. It achieves a worst-case competitive ratio of $3k-2$ while it is optimal when only general requests appear or when specific requests dominate the input sequence. The second has a worst-case competitive ratio of $2k+14$. For the first algorithm, we show a lower bound of $3k-2$, while the second algorithm has a lower bound of $2k-1$ when only general requests appear. The two algorithms differ in only one behavioral rule that significantly influences the competitive ratio. We show that there is a trade-off between performing well against instances of the $k$-Server Problem and mixed instances based on the rule. Additionally, no deterministic online algorithm can be optimal for both kinds of instances simultaneously. Regarding non-uniform metrics, we present an adaption of the Double Coverage algorithm for $2$ servers on the line achieving a competitive ratio of $6$, and an adaption of the Work-Function-Algorithm achieving a competitive ratio of $4k$.
summary: The $k$-Server Problem covers plenty of resource allocation scenarios, and several variations have been studied extensively for decades. We present a model generalizing the $k$-Server Problem by preferences of the requests, where the servers are not identical and requests can express which specific servers should serve them. In our model, requests can either be answered by any server (general requests) or by a specific one (specific requests). If only general requests appear, the instance is one of the original $k$-Server Problem, and a lower bound for the competitive ratio of $k$ applies. If only specific requests appear, a solution with a competitive ratio of $1$ becomes trivial. We show that if both kinds of requests appear, the lower bound raises to $2k-1$. We study deterministic online algorithms and present two algorithms for uniform metrics. The first one has a competitive ratio dependent on the frequency of specific requests. It achieves a worst-case competitive ratio of $3k-2$ while it is optimal when only general requests appear or when specific requests dominate the input sequence. The second has a worst-case competitive ratio of $2k+14$. For the first algorithm, we show a lower bound of $3k-2$, while the second algorithm has a lower bound of $2k-1$ when only general requests appear. The two algorithms differ in only one behavioral rule that significantly influences the competitive ratio. We show that there is a trade-off between performing well against instances of the $k$-Server Problem and mixed instances based on the rule. Additionally, no deterministic online algorithm can be optimal for both kinds of instances simultaneously. Regarding non-uniform metrics, we present an adaption of the Double Coverage algorithm for $2$ servers on the line achieving a competitive ratio of $6$, and an adaption of the Work-Function-Algorithm achieving a competitive ratio of $4k$.
abstract_zh: $k$-服务器问题涵盖了大量的资源分配场景，并且数十年来已经对几种变体进行了广泛研究。我们提出了一个通过请求偏好来概括 $k$ 服务器问题的模型，其中服务器不相同，并且请求可以表达哪些特定服务器应该为它们提供服务。在我们的模型中，请求可以由任何服务器（一般请求）或特定服务器（特定请求）来应答。如果仅出现一般请求，则该实例是原始$k$服务器问题之一，并且适用$k$竞争比的下限。如果只出现特定的请求，那么竞争比为 1 美元的解决方案就变得微不足道。我们表明，如果两种请求都出现，则下限将提高到 $2k-1$。我们研究确定性在线算法并提出两种统一度量的算法。第一个的竞争比率取决于特定请求的频率。它的最坏情况竞争比为 3k-2$，而当仅出现一般请求或特定请求主导输入序列时，它是最佳的。第二个的最坏情况竞争率为 $2k+14$。对于第一个算法，我们显示的下限为 $3k-2$，而当仅出现一般请求时，第二个算法的下限为 $2k-1$。这两种算法仅在一项显着影响竞争比的行为规则上有所不同。我们表明，针对 $k$ 服务器问题的实例和基于规则的混合实例表现良好之间存在权衡。此外，没有任何确定性在线算法可以同时对于两种实例都是最佳的。关于非统一指标，我们提出了对线上 2 美元服务器的双重覆盖算法的适应，实现了 6 美元的竞争比，以及对工作功能算法的适应，实现了 4k 美元的竞争比。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种扩展的k-Server问题模型，考虑请求对服务器的偏好，分析了混合请求下的竞争比下界，并设计了两种确定性在线算法。
AI.motivation: 传统k-Server问题假设服务器完全对等，但实际场景中请求可能偏好特定服务器，需研究此类扩展模型的竞争比及算法性能。
AI.method: 引入包含通用请求和特定请求的混合模型，分析竞争比下界，设计两种针对均匀度量空间的算法，并扩展经典算法到非均匀度量空间。
AI.result: 混合请求下竞争比下界为2k-1，提出两种算法分别达到3k-2和2k+14的竞争比，证明算法性能存在权衡，非均匀度量下改进算法达到6和4k的竞争比。
AI.conclusion: 偏好模型更贴近实际场景，算法性能存在通用实例与混合实例的权衡，改进的经典算法在非均匀度量中表现有效。

authors_text: Sharut Gupta,Kartik Ahuja,Mohammad Havaei,Niladri Chatterjee,Yoshua Bengio
title: FL Games: A federated learning framework for distribution shifts
title_zh: FL Games：分布转移的联合学习框架
abstract: Federated learning aims to train predictive models for data that is distributed across clients, under the orchestration of a server. However, participating clients typically each hold data from a different distribution, whereby predictive models with strong in-distribution generalization can fail catastrophically on unseen domains. In this work, we argue that in order to generalize better across non-i.i.d. clients, it is imperative to only learn correlations that are stable and invariant across domains. We propose FL Games, a game-theoretic framework for federated learning for learning causal features that are invariant across clients. While training to achieve the Nash equilibrium, the traditional best response strategy suffers from high-frequency oscillations. We demonstrate that FL Games effectively resolves this challenge and exhibits smooth performance curves. Further, FL Games scales well in the number of clients, requires significantly fewer communication rounds, and is agnostic to device heterogeneity. Through empirical evaluation, we demonstrate that FL Games achieves high out-of-distribution performance on various benchmarks.
summary: Federated learning aims to train predictive models for data that is distributed across clients, under the orchestration of a server. However, participating clients typically each hold data from a different distribution, whereby predictive models with strong in-distribution generalization can fail catastrophically on unseen domains. In this work, we argue that in order to generalize better across non-i.i.d. clients, it is imperative to only learn correlations that are stable and invariant across domains. We propose FL Games, a game-theoretic framework for federated learning for learning causal features that are invariant across clients. While training to achieve the Nash equilibrium, the traditional best response strategy suffers from high-frequency oscillations. We demonstrate that FL Games effectively resolves this challenge and exhibits smooth performance curves. Further, FL Games scales well in the number of clients, requires significantly fewer communication rounds, and is agnostic to device heterogeneity. Through empirical evaluation, we demonstrate that FL Games achieves high out-of-distribution performance on various benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习旨在在服务器的编排下训练跨客户端分布的数据的预测模型。然而，参与的客户通常都持有来自不同分布的数据，因此具有强分布内泛化能力的预测模型可能会在不可见的领域中发生灾难性的失败。在这项工作中，我们认为为了更好地概括非独立同分布。对于客户来说，必须只学习跨领域稳定且不变的相关性。我们提出了 FL Games，这是一种联邦学习的博弈论框架，用于学习跨客户端不变的因果特征。在训练实现纳什均衡时，传统的最佳响应策略会遭受高频振荡。我们证明FL Games有效地解决了这一挑战并展现出平滑的性能曲线。此外，FL Games 在客户端数量方面具有良好的扩展性，需要的通信次数显着减少，并且不受设备异构性的影响。通过实证评估，我们证明腹灵游戏在各种基准测试中都取得了较高的发行外性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FL Games框架，通过博弈论方法解决联邦学习中非独立同分布数据导致的分布偏移问题。
AI.motivation: 传统联邦学习模型在跨客户端分布差异场景下泛化能力不足，需学习跨域稳定的因果特征以提升分布外性能。
AI.method: 基于博弈论设计FL Games框架，在纳什均衡训练中优化最佳响应策略，通过稳定因果特征学习解决高频震荡问题，并兼容设备异构性。
AI.result: 实验表明FL Games在多个基准测试中实现更优分布外性能，通信轮次显著减少，且能扩展至大量客户端场景。
AI.conclusion: FL Games通过因果不变特征学习有效应对联邦学习中的分布偏移挑战，兼具通信效率与可扩展性优势。

authors_text: Jiangmeng Li,Wenyi Mo,Wenwen Qiang,Bing Su,Changwen Zheng,Hui Xiong,Ji-Rong Wen
title: Supporting Vision-Language Model Inference with Confounder-pruning Knowledge Prompt
title_zh: 通过混杂修剪知识提示支持视觉语言模型推理
abstract: Vision-language models are pre-trained by aligning image-text pairs in a common space to deal with open-set visual concepts. To boost the transferability of the pre-trained models, recent works adopt fixed or learnable prompts, i.e., classification weights are synthesized from natural language describing task-relevant categories, to reduce the gap between tasks in the training and test phases. However, how and what prompts can improve inference performance remains unclear. In this paper, we explicitly clarify the importance of including semantic information in prompts, while existing prompting methods generate prompts without exploring the semantic information of textual labels. Manually constructing prompts with rich semantics requires domain expertise and is extremely time-consuming. To cope with this issue, we propose a semantic-aware prompt learning method, namely CPKP, which retrieves an ontological knowledge graph by treating the textual label as a query to extract task-relevant semantic information. CPKP further introduces a double-tier confounder-pruning procedure to refine the derived semantic information. The graph-tier confounders are gradually identified and phased out, inspired by the principle of Granger causality. The feature-tier confounders are demolished by following the maximum entropy principle in information theory. Empirically, the evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of CPKP, e.g., with two shots, CPKP outperforms the manual-prompt method by 4.64% and the learnable-prompt method by 1.09% on average, and the superiority of CPKP in domain generalization compared to benchmark approaches. Our implementation is available at https://github.com/Mowenyii/CPKP.
summary: Vision-language models are pre-trained by aligning image-text pairs in a common space to deal with open-set visual concepts. To boost the transferability of the pre-trained models, recent works adopt fixed or learnable prompts, i.e., classification weights are synthesized from natural language describing task-relevant categories, to reduce the gap between tasks in the training and test phases. However, how and what prompts can improve inference performance remains unclear. In this paper, we explicitly clarify the importance of including semantic information in prompts, while existing prompting methods generate prompts without exploring the semantic information of textual labels. Manually constructing prompts with rich semantics requires domain expertise and is extremely time-consuming. To cope with this issue, we propose a semantic-aware prompt learning method, namely CPKP, which retrieves an ontological knowledge graph by treating the textual label as a query to extract task-relevant semantic information. CPKP further introduces a double-tier confounder-pruning procedure to refine the derived semantic information. The graph-tier confounders are gradually identified and phased out, inspired by the principle of Granger causality. The feature-tier confounders are demolished by following the maximum entropy principle in information theory. Empirically, the evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of CPKP, e.g., with two shots, CPKP outperforms the manual-prompt method by 4.64% and the learnable-prompt method by 1.09% on average, and the superiority of CPKP in domain generalization compared to benchmark approaches. Our implementation is available at https://github.com/Mowenyii/CPKP.
abstract_zh: 视觉语言模型通过在公共空间中对齐图像-文本对来进行预训练，以处理开放集视觉概念。为了提高预训练模型的可迁移性，最近的工作采用固定或可学习的提示，即从描述任务相关类别的自然语言合成分类权重，以缩小训练和测试阶段任务之间的差距。然而，如何以及什么提示可以提高推理性能仍不清楚。在本文中，我们明确阐明了在提示中包含语义信息的重要性，而现有的提示方法在生成提示时没有探索文本标签的语义信息。手动构建具有丰富语义的提示需要领域专业知识，并且非常耗时。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种语义感知提示学习方法，即 CPKP，它通过将文本标签视为查询来检索本体知识图，以提取与任务相关的语义信息。 CPKP 进一步引入了双层混杂修剪过程来细化导出的语义信息。受格兰杰因果关系原理的启发，图层混杂因素逐渐被识别并逐步淘汰。通过遵循信息论中的最大熵原理，可以消除特征层混杂因素。从经验上看，评估证明了 CPKP 的有效性，例如，在两次射击的情况下，CPKP 平均优于手动提示方法 4.64% 和可学习提示方法 1.09%，并且与基准方法相比，CPKP 在领域泛化方面具有优越性。我们的实现可以在 https://github.com/Mowenyii/CPKP 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种语义感知的提示学习方法CPKP，通过结合本体知识图谱和双重消噪机制，提升视觉-语言模型在开放集概念上的推理性能。
AI.motivation: 现有提示方法未有效利用文本标签的语义信息，且手动构建语义丰富的提示依赖领域知识且耗时，限制了模型迁移能力。
AI.method: CPKP方法以文本标签为查询检索本体知识图谱，提取任务相关语义信息，并通过两阶段消噪：基于格兰杰因果的图层级消噪和信息论最大熵原则的特征层级消噪，优化语义表示。
AI.result: 实验表明，在两样本设置下，CPKP较手动提示方法平均提升4.64%，较可学习提示方法提升1.09%，且在领域泛化能力上优于基准方法。
AI.conclusion: CPKP通过整合本体语义知识与双重消噪机制，有效提升了视觉-语言模型的推理性能与跨领域适应性。

title: PointDistiller: Structured Knowledge Distillation Towards Efficient and Compact 3D Detection
title_zh: PointDistiller：结构化知识蒸馏实现高效、紧凑的 3D 检测
abstract: The remarkable breakthroughs in point cloud representation learning have boosted their usage in real-world applications such as self-driving cars and virtual reality. However, these applications usually have an urgent requirement for not only accurate but also efficient 3D object detection. Recently, knowledge distillation has been proposed as an effective model compression technique, which transfers the knowledge from an over-parameterized teacher to a lightweight student and achieves consistent effectiveness in 2D vision. However, due to point clouds' sparsity and irregularity, directly applying previous image-based knowledge distillation methods to point cloud detectors usually leads to unsatisfactory performance. To fill the gap, this paper proposes PointDistiller, a structured knowledge distillation framework for point clouds-based 3D detection. Concretely, PointDistiller includes local distillation which extracts and distills the local geometric structure of point clouds with dynamic graph convolution and reweighted learning strategy, which highlights student learning on the crucial points or voxels to improve knowledge distillation efficiency. Extensive experiments on both voxels-based and raw points-based detectors have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method over seven previous knowledge distillation methods. For instance, our 4X compressed PointPillars student achieves 2.8 and 3.4 mAP improvements on BEV and 3D object detection, outperforming its teacher by 0.9 and 1.8 mAP, respectively. Codes have been released at https://github.com/RunpeiDong/PointDistiller.

Concretely, PointDistiller includes local distillation which extracts and distills the local geometric structure of point clouds with dynamic graph convolution and reweighted learning strategy, which highlights student learning on the crucial points or voxels to improve knowledge distillation efficiency. Extensive experiments on both voxels-based and raw points-based detectors have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method over seven previous knowledge distillation methods. For instance, our 4X compressed PointPillars student achieves 2.8 and 3.4 mAP improvements on BEV and 3D object detection, outperforming its teacher by 0.9 and 1.8 mAP, respectively. Codes have been released at https://github.com/RunpeiDong/PointDistiller.
abstract_zh: 点云表示学习的显着突破促进了它们在自动驾驶汽车和虚拟现实等现实世界应用中的使用。然而，这些应用通常迫切需要准确且高效的 3D 物体检测。最近，知识蒸馏被提出作为一种有效的模型压缩技术，它将知识从过度参数化的教师转移到轻量级的学生，并在 2D 视觉中实现一致的有效性。然而，由于点云的稀疏性和不规则性，直接将先前基于图像的知识蒸馏方法应用于点云检测器通常会导致性能不理想。为了填补这一空白，本文提出了 PointDistiller，一种用于基于点云的 3D 检测的结构化知识蒸馏框架。具体来说，PointDistiller包括局部蒸馏，通过动态图卷积和重新加权学习策略来提取和蒸馏点云的局部几何结构，突出学生对关键点或体素的学习，以提高知识蒸馏效率。基于体素和基于原始点的检测器的广泛实验证明了我们的方法比以前的七种知识蒸馏方法的有效性。例如，我们的 4 倍压缩 PointPillars 学生在 BEV 和 3D 对象检测上实现了 2.8 和 3.4 mAP 改进，分别比老师高出 0.9 和 1.8 mAP。代码已发布于 https://github.com/RunpeiDong/PointDistiller。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PointDistiller，一种面向点云的结构化知识蒸馏框架，旨在提升3D目标检测的效率和精度。
AI.motivation: 现有基于图像的知识蒸馏方法难以直接应用于点云检测，因其稀疏性和不规则性导致性能不佳，需设计适合点云特性的蒸馏方法。
AI.method: PointDistiller通过动态图卷积和重加权学习策略，提取点云局部几何结构进行局部蒸馏，使学生模型聚焦关键点或体素以提升蒸馏效率。
AI.result: 在基于体素和原始点云的检测器上，PointDistiller相比七种先前方法表现更优，4倍压缩的PointPillars学生模型在BEV和3D检测中分别提升2.8/3.4 mAP，超越教师模型0.9/1.8 mAP。
AI.conclusion: PointDistiller通过结构化知识蒸馏有效压缩3D检测模型，同时保持高精度，验证了其在点云场景下的有效性。

search_text: PointDistiller: Structured Knowledge Distillation Towards Efficient and Compact 3D Detection PointDistiller：结构化知识蒸馏实现高效、紧凑的 3D 检测 The remarkable breakthroughs in point cloud representation learning have boosted their usage in real-world applications such as self-driving cars and virtual reality. However, these applications usually have an urgent requirement for not only accurate but also efficient 3D object detection. Recently, knowledge distillation has been proposed as an effective model compression technique, which transfers the knowledge from an over-parameterized teacher to a lightweight student and achieves consistent effectiveness in 2D vision. However, due to point clouds' sparsity and irregularity, directly applying previous image-based knowledge distillation methods to point cloud detectors usually leads to unsatisfactory performance. To fill the gap, this paper proposes PointDistiller, a structured knowledge distillation framework for point clouds-based 3D detection. Concretely, PointDistiller includes local distillation which extracts and distills the local geometric structure of point clouds with dynamic graph convolution and reweighted learning strategy, which highlights student learning on the crucial points or voxels to improve knowledge distillation efficiency. Extensive experiments on both voxels-based and raw points-based detectors have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method over seven previous knowledge distillation methods. For instance, our 4X compressed PointPillars student achieves 2.8 and 3.4 mAP improvements on BEV and 3D object detection, outperforming its teacher by 0.9 and 1.8 mAP, respectively. Codes have been released at https://github.com/RunpeiDong/PointDistiller. 点云表示学习的显着突破促进了它们在自动驾驶汽车和虚拟现实等现实世界应用中的使用。然而，这些应用通常迫切需要准确且高效的 3D 物体检测。最近，知识蒸馏被提出作为一种有效的模型压缩技术，它将知识从过度参数化的教师转移到轻量级的学生，并在 2D 视觉中实现一致的有效性。然而，由于点云的稀疏性和不规则性，直接将先前基于图像的知识蒸馏方法应用于点云检测器通常会导致性能不理想。为了填补这一空白，本文提出了 PointDistiller，一种用于基于点云的 3D 检测的结构化知识蒸馏框架。具体来说，PointDistiller包括局部蒸馏，通过动态图卷积和重新加权学习策略来提取和蒸馏点云的局部几何结构，突出学生对关键点或体素的学习，以提高知识蒸馏效率。基于体素和基于原始点的检测器的广泛实验证明了我们的方法比以前的七种知识蒸馏方法的有效性。例如，我们的 4 倍压缩 PointPillars 学生在 BEV 和 3D 对象检测上实现了 2.8 和 3.4 mAP 改进，分别比老师高出 0.9 和 1.8 mAP。代码已发布于 https://github.com/RunpeiDong/PointDistiller。 本文提出PointDistiller，一种面向点云的结构化知识蒸馏框架，旨在提升3D目标检测的效率和精度。 Linfeng Zhang,Runpei Dong,Hung-Shuo Tai,Kaisheng Ma cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Tobias Bernecker,Annette Peters,Christopher L. Schlett,Fabian Bamberg,Fabian Theis,Daniel Rueckert,Jakob Weiß,Shadi Albarqouni
title: FedNorm: Modality-Based Normalization in Federated Learning for Multi-Modal Liver Segmentation
title_zh: FedNorm：多模态肝脏分割联合学习中基于模态的标准化
abstract: Given the high incidence and effective treatment options for liver diseases, they are of great socioeconomic importance. One of the most common methods for analyzing CT and MRI images for diagnosis and follow-up treatment is liver segmentation. Recent advances in deep learning have demonstrated encouraging results for automatic liver segmentation. Despite this, their success depends primarily on the availability of an annotated database, which is often not available because of privacy concerns. Federated Learning has been recently proposed as a solution to alleviate these challenges by training a shared global model on distributed clients without access to their local databases. Nevertheless, Federated Learning does not perform well when it is trained on a high degree of heterogeneity of image data due to multi-modal imaging, such as CT and MRI, and multiple scanner types. To this end, we propose Fednorm and its extension \fednormp, two Federated Learning algorithms that use a modality-based normalization technique. Specifically, Fednorm normalizes the features on a client-level, while Fednorm+ employs the modality information of single slices in the feature normalization. Our methods were validated using 428 patients from six publicly available databases and compared to state-of-the-art Federated Learning algorithms and baseline models in heterogeneous settings (multi-institutional, multi-modal data). The experimental results demonstrate that our methods show an overall acceptable performance, achieve Dice per patient scores up to 0.961, consistently outperform locally trained models, and are on par or slightly better than centralized models.
summary: Given the high incidence and effective treatment options for liver diseases, they are of great socioeconomic importance. One of the most common methods for analyzing CT and MRI images for diagnosis and follow-up treatment is liver segmentation. Recent advances in deep learning have demonstrated encouraging results for automatic liver segmentation. Despite this, their success depends primarily on the availability of an annotated database, which is often not available because of privacy concerns. Federated Learning has been recently proposed as a solution to alleviate these challenges by training a shared global model on distributed clients without access to their local databases. Nevertheless, Federated Learning does not perform well when it is trained on a high degree of heterogeneity of image data due to multi-modal imaging, such as CT and MRI, and multiple scanner types. To this end, we propose Fednorm and its extension \fednormp, two Federated Learning algorithms that use a modality-based normalization technique. Specifically, Fednorm normalizes the features on a client-level, while Fednorm+ employs the modality information of single slices in the feature normalization. Our methods were validated using 428 patients from six publicly available databases and compared to state-of-the-art Federated Learning algorithms and baseline models in heterogeneous settings (multi-institutional, multi-modal data). The experimental results demonstrate that our methods show an overall acceptable performance, achieve Dice per patient scores up to 0.961, consistently outperform locally trained models, and are on par or slightly better than centralized models.
abstract_zh: 鉴于肝病的高发病率和有效的治疗选择，它们具有重大的社会经济重要性。分析 CT 和 MRI 图像以进行诊断和后续治疗的最常用方法之一是肝脏分割。深度学习的最新进展已经证明自动肝脏分割取得了令人鼓舞的结果。尽管如此，他们的成功主要取决于注释数据库的可用性，而由于隐私问题，该数据库通常不可用。最近提出了联合学习作为一种解决方案，通过在分布式客户端上训练共享的全局模型而无需访问其本地数据库来缓解这些挑战。然而，由于多模态成像（例如 CT 和 MRI）以及多种扫描仪类型，联邦学习在高度异质性的图像数据上进行训练时表现不佳。为此，我们提出 Fednorm 及其扩展 \fednormp，这两种使用基于模态的标准化技术的联邦学习算法。具体来说，Fednorm 在客户端级别对特征进行归一化，而 Fednorm+ 在特征归一化中使用单个切片的模态信息。我们的方法使用来自 6 个公开数据库的 428 名患者进行了验证，并与异构环境（多机构、多模式数据）中最先进的联邦学习算法和基线模型进行了比较。实验结果表明，我们的方法表现出总体可接受的性能，每个患者的 Dice 得分高达 0.961，始终优于本地训练的模型，并且与集中式模型相当或略好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedNorm和FedNorm+算法，通过模态感知的归一化技术解决联邦学习中多模态肝脏分割的数据异质性问题。
AI.motivation: 肝脏分割依赖标注数据，但隐私限制导致数据无法集中。联邦学习虽可分布式训练，但面对CT/MRI等多模态医学影像的异质性表现不佳。
AI.method: FedNorm在客户端进行特征归一化，FedNorm+进一步利用单层切片的模态信息优化归一化过程，通过多中心数据库验证有效性。
AI.result: 在428例多中心数据上达到0.961的Dice分数，优于本地模型且接近中心化模型，证明方法在异质环境下有效性。
AI.conclusion: 模态感知归一化显著提升联邦学习在多模态医学影像分割中的性能，为隐私保护下的联合建模提供可行方案。

authors_text: Kai Wang,Bo Zhao,Xiangyu Peng,Zheng Zhu,Jiankang Deng,Xinchao Wang,Hakan Bilen,Yang You
title: FaceMAE: Privacy-Preserving Face Recognition via Masked Autoencoders
title_zh: FaceMAE：通过屏蔽自动编码器进行隐私保护人脸识别
comment: A new paradigm for privacy-preserving face recognition via MAE
comments: A new paradigm for privacy-preserving face recognition via MAE
abstract: Face recognition, as one of the most successful applications in artificial intelligence, has been widely used in security, administration, advertising, and healthcare. However, the privacy issues of public face datasets have attracted increasing attention in recent years. Previous works simply mask most areas of faces or synthesize samples using generative models to construct privacy-preserving face datasets, which overlooks the trade-off between privacy protection and data utility. In this paper, we propose a novel framework FaceMAE, where the face privacy and recognition performance are considered simultaneously. Firstly, randomly masked face images are used to train the reconstruction module in FaceMAE. We tailor the instance relation matching (IRM) module to minimize the distribution gap between real faces and FaceMAE reconstructed ones. During the deployment phase, we use trained FaceMAE to reconstruct images from masked faces of unseen identities without extra training. The risk of privacy leakage is measured based on face retrieval between reconstructed and original datasets. Experiments prove that the identities of reconstructed images are difficult to be retrieved. We also perform sufficient privacy-preserving face recognition on several public face datasets (i.e. CASIA-WebFace and WebFace260M). Compared to previous state of the arts, FaceMAE consistently \textbf{reduces at least 50\% error rate} on LFW, CFP-FP and AgeDB.

We also perform sufficient privacy-preserving face recognition on several public face datasets (i.e. CASIA-WebFace and WebFace260M). Compared to previous state of the arts, FaceMAE consistently \textbf{reduces at least 50\% error rate} on LFW, CFP-FP and AgeDB.
abstract_zh: 人脸识别作为人工智能最成功的应用之一，已广泛应用于安全、行政、广告、医疗等领域。然而，近年来，公共人脸数据集的隐私问题引起了越来越多的关注。以往的工作只是简单地掩盖人脸的大部分区域或使用生成模型合成样本来构建保护隐私的人脸数据集，忽略了隐私保护和数据效用之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的框架 FaceMAE，其中同时考虑了面部隐私和识别性能。首先，使用随机掩模的人脸图像来训练 FaceMAE 中的重建模块。我们定制了实例关系匹配（IRM）模块，以最小化真实人脸和 FaceMAE 重建人脸之间的分布差距。在部署阶段，我们使用训练有素的 FaceMAE 从未见过的身份的蒙面面孔中重建图像，而无需额外的训练。隐私泄露的风险是基于重建数据集和原始数据集之间的人脸检索来衡量的。实验证明重建图像的身份很难恢复。我们还在几个公共人脸数据集（即 CASIA-WebFace 和 WebFace260M）上进行了足够的隐私保护人脸识别。与之前的技术水平相比，FaceMAE 在 LFW、CFP-FP 和 AgeDB 上始终\textbf{减少了至少 50\% 的错误率}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于掩码自编码器的隐私保护人脸识别框架FaceMAE，通过同时考虑隐私保护与数据效用来解决公共人脸数据集的隐私问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通过简单遮挡或生成模型构建隐私保护数据集，但忽视了隐私保护与数据效用之间的平衡，导致隐私泄露风险与识别性能难以兼顾。
AI.method: FaceMAE采用随机遮挡的人脸图像训练重建模块，并通过实例关系匹配（IRM）模块缩小真实人脸与重建人脸之间的分布差距，部署时无需额外训练即可从遮挡人脸中重建图像。
AI.result: 实验表明，重建图像难以被检索到原始身份，且在CASIA-WebFace和WebFace260M等数据集上，FaceMAE在LFW、CFP-FP和AgeDB测试集上将错误率降低至少50%。
AI.conclusion: FaceMAE通过同时优化隐私保护与识别性能，有效降低了隐私泄露风险，并在多个基准数据集上显著优于现有方法。

search_text: FaceMAE: Privacy-Preserving Face Recognition via Masked Autoencoders FaceMAE：通过屏蔽自动编码器进行隐私保护人脸识别 Face recognition, as one of the most successful applications in artificial intelligence, has been widely used in security, administration, advertising, and healthcare. However, the privacy issues of public face datasets have attracted increasing attention in recent years. Previous works simply mask most areas of faces or synthesize samples using generative models to construct privacy-preserving face datasets, which overlooks the trade-off between privacy protection and data utility. In this paper, we propose a novel framework FaceMAE, where the face privacy and recognition performance are considered simultaneously. Firstly, randomly masked face images are used to train the reconstruction module in FaceMAE. We tailor the instance relation matching (IRM) module to minimize the distribution gap between real faces and FaceMAE reconstructed ones. During the deployment phase, we use trained FaceMAE to reconstruct images from masked faces of unseen identities without extra training. The risk of privacy leakage is measured based on face retrieval between reconstructed and original datasets. Experiments prove that the identities of reconstructed images are difficult to be retrieved. We also perform sufficient privacy-preserving face recognition on several public face datasets (i.e. CASIA-WebFace and WebFace260M). Compared to previous state of the arts, FaceMAE consistently \textbf{reduces at least 50\% error rate} on LFW, CFP-FP and AgeDB. 人脸识别作为人工智能最成功的应用之一，已广泛应用于安全、行政、广告、医疗等领域。然而，近年来，公共人脸数据集的隐私问题引起了越来越多的关注。以往的工作只是简单地掩盖人脸的大部分区域或使用生成模型合成样本来构建保护隐私的人脸数据集，忽略了隐私保护和数据效用之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的框架 FaceMAE，其中同时考虑了面部隐私和识别性能。首先，使用随机掩模的人脸图像来训练 FaceMAE 中的重建模块。我们定制了实例关系匹配（IRM）模块，以最小化真实人脸和 FaceMAE 重建人脸之间的分布差距。在部署阶段，我们使用训练有素的 FaceMAE 从未见过的身份的蒙面面孔中重建图像，而无需额外的训练。隐私泄露的风险是基于重建数据集和原始数据集之间的人脸检索来衡量的。实验证明重建图像的身份很难恢复。我们还在几个公共人脸数据集（即 CASIA-WebFace 和 WebFace260M）上进行了足够的隐私保护人脸识别。与之前的技术水平相比，FaceMAE 在 LFW、CFP-FP 和 AgeDB 上始终\textbf{减少了至少 50\% 的错误率}。 本文提出一种基于掩码自编码器的隐私保护人脸识别框架FaceMAE，通过同时考虑隐私保护与数据效用来解决公共人脸数据集的隐私问题。 Kai Wang,Bo Zhao,Xiangyu Peng,Zheng Zhu,Jiankang Deng,Xinchao Wang,Hakan Bilen,Yang You cs.CV

title: Deep Digging into the Generalization of Self-Supervised Monocular Depth Estimation
title_zh: 深入挖掘自监督单目深度估计的推广
abstract: Self-supervised monocular depth estimation has been widely studied recently. Most of the work has focused on improving performance on benchmark datasets, such as KITTI, but has offered a few experiments on generalization performance. In this paper, we investigate the backbone networks (e.g. CNNs, Transformers, and CNN-Transformer hybrid models) toward the generalization of monocular depth estimation. We first evaluate state-of-the-art models on diverse public datasets, which have never been seen during the network training. Next, we investigate the effects of texture-biased and shape-biased representations using the various texture-shifted datasets that we generated. We observe that Transformers exhibit a strong shape bias and CNNs do a strong texture-bias. We also find that shape-biased models show better generalization performance for monocular depth estimation compared to texture-biased models. Based on these observations, we newly design a CNN-Transformer hybrid network with a multi-level adaptive feature fusion module, called MonoFormer. The design intuition behind MonoFormer is to increase shape bias by employing Transformers while compensating for the weak locality bias of Transformers by adaptively fusing multi-level representations. Extensive experiments show that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance with various public datasets. Our method also shows the best generalization ability among the competitive methods.
summary: Self-supervised monocular depth estimation has been widely studied recently. Most of the work has focused on improving performance on benchmark datasets, such as KITTI, but has offered a few experiments on generalization performance. In this paper, we investigate the backbone networks (e.g. CNNs, Transformers, and CNN-Transformer hybrid models) toward the generalization of monocular depth estimation. We first evaluate state-of-the-art models on diverse public datasets, which have never been seen during the network training. Next, we investigate the effects of texture-biased and shape-biased representations using the various texture-shifted datasets that we generated. We observe that Transformers exhibit a strong shape bias and CNNs do a strong texture-bias. We also find that shape-biased models show better generalization performance for monocular depth estimation compared to texture-biased models. Based on these observations, we newly design a CNN-Transformer hybrid network with a multi-level adaptive feature fusion module, called MonoFormer. The design intuition behind MonoFormer is to increase shape bias by employing Transformers while compensating for the weak locality bias of Transformers by adaptively fusing multi-level representations. Extensive experiments show that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance with various public datasets. Our method also shows the best generalization ability among the competitive methods.
abstract_zh: 自监督单目深度估计最近得到了广泛的研究。大多数工作都集中在提高基准数据集（例如 KITTI）的性能上，但也提供了一些关于泛化性能的实验。在本文中，我们研究了主干网络（例如 CNN、Transformer 和 CNN-Transformer 混合模型），以实现单目深度估计的推广。我们首先在不同的公共数据集上评估最先进的模型，这些模型在网络训练期间从未见过。接下来，我们使用我们生成的各种纹理移位数据集研究纹理偏置和形状偏置表示的效果。我们观察到 Transformer 表现出强烈的形状偏差，而 CNN 表现出强烈的纹理偏差。我们还发现，与纹理偏置模型相比，形状偏置模型在单眼深度估计方面表现出更好的泛化性能。基于这些观察，我们新设计了一个具有多级自适应特征融合模块的 CNN-Transformer 混合网络，称为 MonoFormer。 MonoFormer 背后的设计直觉是通过使用 Transformer 来增加形状偏差，同时通过自适应融合多级表示来补偿 Transformer 的弱局部性偏差。大量实验表明，所提出的方法在各种公共数据集上实现了最先进的性能。我们的方法还显示出竞争方法中最好的泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了自监督单目深度估计模型在不同数据集上的泛化能力，比较了CNN、Transformer及混合模型的性能差异，并提出了一种新的混合网络MonoFormer。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多聚焦于基准数据集性能提升，但缺乏对模型泛化能力的系统性分析。作者旨在探索不同网络结构（如CNN、Transformer）对泛化能力的影响，并解决纹理偏差与形状偏差带来的性能差异问题。
AI.method: 通过在未见过的公开数据集上评估现有模型，生成纹理偏移数据集分析纹理/形状表示的影响，发现Transformer具有强形状偏差而CNN具有强纹理偏差。基于此设计融合多级特征的CNN-Transformer混合网络MonoFormer，通过自适应特征融合增强形状偏差并补偿Transformer的局部性不足。
AI.result: MonoFormer在多个公开数据集上达到SOTA性能，且在竞争方法中展现出最优的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 形状偏差模型（如Transformer）在单目深度估计中具有更好的泛化性，而结合CNN局部性优势的混合架构（MonoFormer）能进一步提升性能与泛化能力。

authors_text: Fanfan Ye,Liang Ma,Qiaoyong Zhong,Di Xie,Shiliang Pu
title: Self-distilled Knowledge Delegator for Exemplar-free Class Incremental Learning
title_zh: 自蒸馏知识委托器，实现无范例课堂增量学习
abstract: Exemplar-free incremental learning is extremely challenging due to inaccessibility of data from old tasks. In this paper, we attempt to exploit the knowledge encoded in a previously trained classification model to handle the catastrophic forgetting problem in continual learning. Specifically, we introduce a so-called knowledge delegator, which is capable of transferring knowledge from the trained model to a randomly re-initialized new model by generating informative samples. Given the previous model only, the delegator is effectively learned using a self-distillation mechanism in a data-free manner. The knowledge extracted by the delegator is then utilized to maintain the performance of the model on old tasks in incremental learning. This simple incremental learning framework surpasses existing exemplar-free methods by a large margin on four widely used class incremental benchmarks, namely CIFAR-100, ImageNet-Subset, Caltech-101 and Flowers-102. Notably, we achieve comparable performance to some exemplar-based methods without accessing any exemplars.
summary: Exemplar-free incremental learning is extremely challenging due to inaccessibility of data from old tasks. In this paper, we attempt to exploit the knowledge encoded in a previously trained classification model to handle the catastrophic forgetting problem in continual learning. Specifically, we introduce a so-called knowledge delegator, which is capable of transferring knowledge from the trained model to a randomly re-initialized new model by generating informative samples. Given the previous model only, the delegator is effectively learned using a self-distillation mechanism in a data-free manner. The knowledge extracted by the delegator is then utilized to maintain the performance of the model on old tasks in incremental learning. This simple incremental learning framework surpasses existing exemplar-free methods by a large margin on four widely used class incremental benchmarks, namely CIFAR-100, ImageNet-Subset, Caltech-101 and Flowers-102. Notably, we achieve comparable performance to some exemplar-based methods without accessing any exemplars.
abstract_zh: 由于无法访问旧任务中的数据，无样本增量学习极具挑战性。在本文中，我们尝试利用先前训练的分类模型中编码的知识来处理持续学习中的灾难性遗忘问题。具体来说，我们引入了一个所谓的知识委托器，它能够通过生成信息样本将知识从训练好的模型转移到随机重新初始化的新模型。仅考虑到先前的模型，可以使用自蒸馏机制以无数据的方式有效地学习委托人。然后利用委托者提取的知识来维持增量学习中模型在旧任务上的性能。这个简单的增量学习框架在四个广泛使用的类增量基准（即 CIFAR-100、ImageNet-Subset、Caltech-101 和 Flowers-102）上大幅超越了现有的无样本方法。值得注意的是，我们在不访问任何样本的情况下实现了与某些基于样本的方法相当的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需示例的类增量学习框架，通过知识委托机制解决灾难性遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 传统增量学习依赖旧任务数据或示例，但实际场景中这些数据往往不可用，且存储成本高。
AI.method: 设计知识委托器从预训练模型中提取知识，通过自蒸馏机制生成信息样本，无需旧数据即可训练新模型。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100等四个基准测试中显著优于现有无示例方法，性能接近部分基于示例的方法。
AI.conclusion: 该框架证明了知识蒸馏在无示例增量学习中的有效性，为持续学习提供了新思路。

search_text: Self-distilled Knowledge Delegator for Exemplar-free Class Incremental Learning 自蒸馏知识委托器，实现无范例课堂增量学习 Exemplar-free incremental learning is extremely challenging due to inaccessibility of data from old tasks. In this paper, we attempt to exploit the knowledge encoded in a previously trained classification model to handle the catastrophic forgetting problem in continual learning. Specifically, we introduce a so-called knowledge delegator, which is capable of transferring knowledge from the trained model to a randomly re-initialized new model by generating informative samples. Given the previous model only, the delegator is effectively learned using a self-distillation mechanism in a data-free manner. The knowledge extracted by the delegator is then utilized to maintain the performance of the model on old tasks in incremental learning. This simple incremental learning framework surpasses existing exemplar-free methods by a large margin on four widely used class incremental benchmarks, namely CIFAR-100, ImageNet-Subset, Caltech-101 and Flowers-102. Notably, we achieve comparable performance to some exemplar-based methods without accessing any exemplars. 由于无法访问旧任务中的数据，无样本增量学习极具挑战性。在本文中，我们尝试利用先前训练的分类模型中编码的知识来处理持续学习中的灾难性遗忘问题。具体来说，我们引入了一个所谓的知识委托器，它能够通过生成信息样本将知识从训练好的模型转移到随机重新初始化的新模型。仅考虑到先前的模型，可以使用自蒸馏机制以无数据的方式有效地学习委托人。然后利用委托者提取的知识来维持增量学习中模型在旧任务上的性能。这个简单的增量学习框架在四个广泛使用的类增量基准（即 CIFAR-100、ImageNet-Subset、Caltech-101 和 Flowers-102）上大幅超越了现有的无样本方法。值得注意的是，我们在不访问任何样本的情况下实现了与某些基于样本的方法相当的性能。 本文提出一种无需示例的类增量学习框架，通过知识委托机制解决灾难性遗忘问题。 Fanfan Ye,Liang Ma,Qiaoyong Zhong,Di Xie,Shiliang Pu cs.CV

title: WOGAN at the SBST 2022 CPS Tool Competition
title_zh: WOGAN 参加 SBST 2022 CPS 工具大赛
abstract: WOGAN is an online test generation algorithm based on Wasserstein generative adversarial networks. In this note, we present how WOGAN works and summarize its performance in the SBST 2022 CPS tool competition concerning the AI of a self-driving car.
summary: WOGAN is an online test generation algorithm based on Wasserstein generative adversarial networks. In this note, we present how WOGAN works and summarize its performance in the SBST 2022 CPS tool competition concerning the AI of a self-driving car.
abstract_zh: WOGAN 是一种基于 Wasserstein 生成对抗网络的在线测试生成算法。在这篇文章中，我们介绍了 WOGAN 的工作原理，并总结了其在 SBST 2022 CPS 工具竞赛中有关自动驾驶汽车人工智能的表现。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了基于Wasserstein生成对抗网络的在线测试生成算法WOGAN，并总结了其在SBST 2022 CPS工具竞赛中的表现。
AI.motivation: 为了提高自动驾驶汽车AI系统的测试效率和覆盖率，需要一种高效的在线测试生成算法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种基于Wasserstein生成对抗网络（WGAN）的在线测试生成算法WOGAN。
AI.result: WOGAN在SBST 2022 CPS工具竞赛中展示了其在生成测试用例方面的性能。
AI.conclusion: WOGAN作为一种基于生成对抗网络的测试生成方法，在自动驾驶汽车AI系统的测试中具有良好的应用前景。

authors_text: Ehtesham Iqbal,Asim Niaz,Asif Aziz Memon,Usman Asim,Kwang Nam Choi
title: Saliency-Driven Active Contour Model for Image Segmentation
title_zh: 用于图像分割的显着性驱动的主动轮廓模型
abstract: Active contour models have achieved prominent success in the area of image segmentation, allowing complex objects to be segmented from the background for further analysis. Existing models can be divided into region-based active contour models and edge-based active contour models. However, both models use direct image data to achieve segmentation and face many challenging problems in terms of the initial contour position, noise sensitivity, local minima and inefficiency owing to the in-homogeneity of image intensities. The saliency map of an image changes the image representation, making it more visual and meaningful. In this study, we propose a novel model that uses the advantages of a saliency map with local image information (LIF) and overcomes the drawbacks of previous models. The proposed model is driven by a saliency map of an image and the local image information to enhance the progress of the active contour models. In this model, the saliency map of an image is first computed to find the saliency driven local fitting energy. Then, the saliency-driven local fitting energy is combined with the LIF model, resulting in a final novel energy functional. This final energy functional is formulated through a level set formulation, and regulation terms are added to evolve the contour more precisely across the object boundaries. The quality of the proposed method was verified on different synthetic images, real images and publicly available datasets, including medical images. The image segmentation results, and quantitative comparisons confirmed the contour initialization independence, noise insensitivity, and superior segmentation accuracy of the proposed model in comparison to the other segmentation models.
summary: Active contour models have achieved prominent success in the area of image segmentation, allowing complex objects to be segmented from the background for further analysis. Existing models can be divided into region-based active contour models and edge-based active contour models. However, both models use direct image data to achieve segmentation and face many challenging problems in terms of the initial contour position, noise sensitivity, local minima and inefficiency owing to the in-homogeneity of image intensities. The saliency map of an image changes the image representation, making it more visual and meaningful. In this study, we propose a novel model that uses the advantages of a saliency map with local image information (LIF) and overcomes the drawbacks of previous models. The proposed model is driven by a saliency map of an image and the local image information to enhance the progress of the active contour models. In this model, the saliency map of an image is first computed to find the saliency driven local fitting energy. Then, the saliency-driven local fitting energy is combined with the LIF model, resulting in a final novel energy functional. This final energy functional is formulated through a level set formulation, and regulation terms are added to evolve the contour more precisely across the object boundaries. The quality of the proposed method was verified on different synthetic images, real images and publicly available datasets, including medical images. The image segmentation results, and quantitative comparisons confirmed the contour initialization independence, noise insensitivity, and superior segmentation accuracy of the proposed model in comparison to the other segmentation models.
abstract_zh: 主动轮廓模型在图像分割领域取得了显着的成功，允许将复杂的对象从背景中分割出来以进行进一步分析。现有模型可分为基于区域的主动轮廓模型和基于边缘的主动轮廓模型。然而，这两种模型都使用直接图像数据来实现分割，并且由于图像强度不均匀而在初始轮廓位置、噪声敏感性、局部极小值和低效率方面面临许多具有挑战性的问题。图像的显着图改变了图像的表示方式，使其更加直观和有意义。在本研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的模型，该模型利用具有局部图像信息（LIF）的显着图的优点，并克服了先前模型的缺点。所提出的模型由图像的显着图和局部图像信息驱动，以增强活动轮廓模型的进度。在该模型中，首先计算图像的显着性图以找到显着性驱动的局部拟合能量。然后，将显着性驱动的局部拟合能量与 LIF 模型相结合，产生最终的新颖能量泛函。最终的能量泛函通过水平集公式制定，并添加调节项以更精确地跨对象边界演化轮廓。该方法的质量在不同的合成图像、真实图像和公开数据集（包括医学图像）上得到了验证。图像分割结果和定量比较证实了该模型与其他分割模型相比具有轮廓初始化独立性、噪声不敏感性和优越的分割精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于显著性驱动的活动轮廓模型，用于改进图像分割效果。
AI.motivation: 现有活动轮廓模型在初始轮廓位置、噪声敏感性、局部极小值和非均匀强度下的效率等方面存在挑战，需结合显著性图与局部信息优化分割性能。
AI.method: 通过计算图像显著性图获取显著性驱动的局部拟合能量，结合局部图像信息模型（LIF），利用水平集公式构建新型能量泛函并添加调控项优化轮廓演化。
AI.result: 在合成图像、真实图像及医学图像数据集上验证了方法有效性，证明其具有初始化独立性、抗噪能力和更高的分割精度。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过显著性驱动机制有效解决了传统活动轮廓模型的局限性，在分割准确性与鲁棒性方面优于现有方法。

title: Vegetation Mapping by UAV Visible Imagery and Machine Learning
title_zh: 通过无人机可见图像和机器学习进行植被测绘
abstract: An experimental field cropped with sugar-beet with a wide spreading of weeds has been used to test vegetation identification from drone visible imagery. Expert masked and hue-filtered pictures have been used to train several Machine Learning algorithms to develop a semi-automatic methodology for identification and mapping species at high resolution. Results show that 5m altitude allows for obtaining maps with an identification efficiency of more than 90%. Such a method can be easily integrated to present VRHA, as much as tools to obtain detailed maps of vegetation.
summary: An experimental field cropped with sugar-beet with a wide spreading of weeds has been used to test vegetation identification from drone visible imagery. Expert masked and hue-filtered pictures have been used to train several Machine Learning algorithms to develop a semi-automatic methodology for identification and mapping species at high resolution. Results show that 5m altitude allows for obtaining maps with an identification efficiency of more than 90%. Such a method can be easily integrated to present VRHA, as much as tools to obtain detailed maps of vegetation.
abstract_zh: 一片长满杂草的甜菜试验田已被用来测试无人机可见图像的植被识别。专家蒙版和色调过滤图片已用于训练多种机器学习算法，以开发一种半自动方法来识别和绘制高分辨率物种图。结果表明，5m高度下获取地图的识别效率可达90%以上。这种方法可以很容易地集成到当前的 VRHA 中，就像获取详细植被地图的工具一样。

AI.tldr: 利用无人机可见光影像与机器学习方法实现高分辨率植被分类与制图
AI.motivation: 开发高效精准的植被识别方法以满足农业管理需求
AI.method: 采用专家标注与色调过滤图像训练多种机器学习模型，结合无人机可见光影像进行植被识别
AI.result: 5米飞行高度下植被识别效率达90%以上，可生成高精度植被分布图
AI.conclusion: 该方法可有效集成至现有农业系统，为植被监测提供高精度解决方案

authors_text: Yunzhi Lin,Jonathan Tremblay,Stephen Tyree,Patricio A. Vela,Stan Birchfield
title: Keypoint-Based Category-Level Object Pose Tracking from an RGB Sequence with Uncertainty Estimation
title_zh: 基于 RGB 序列的基于关键点的类别级对象姿态跟踪和不确定性估计
abstract: We propose a single-stage, category-level 6-DoF pose estimation algorithm that simultaneously detects and tracks instances of objects within a known category. Our method takes as input the previous and current frame from a monocular RGB video, as well as predictions from the previous frame, to predict the bounding cuboid and 6-DoF pose (up to scale). Internally, a deep network predicts distributions over object keypoints (vertices of the bounding cuboid) in image coordinates, after which a novel probabilistic filtering process integrates across estimates before computing the final pose using PnP. Our framework allows the system to take previous uncertainties into consideration when predicting the current frame, resulting in predictions that are more accurate and stable than single frame methods. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms existing approaches on the challenging Objectron benchmark of annotated object videos. We also demonstrate the usability of our work in an augmented reality setting.
summary: We propose a single-stage, category-level 6-DoF pose estimation algorithm that simultaneously detects and tracks instances of objects within a known category. Our method takes as input the previous and current frame from a monocular RGB video, as well as predictions from the previous frame, to predict the bounding cuboid and 6-DoF pose (up to scale). Internally, a deep network predicts distributions over object keypoints (vertices of the bounding cuboid) in image coordinates, after which a novel probabilistic filtering process integrates across estimates before computing the final pose using PnP. Our framework allows the system to take previous uncertainties into consideration when predicting the current frame, resulting in predictions that are more accurate and stable than single frame methods. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms existing approaches on the challenging Objectron benchmark of annotated object videos. We also demonstrate the usability of our work in an augmented reality setting.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种单阶段、类别级 6-DoF 姿态估计算法，该算法可同时检测和跟踪已知类别内的对象实例。我们的方法将单目 RGB 视频中的前一帧和当前帧以及前一帧的预测作为输入，以预测边界长方体和 6-DoF 姿势（按比例）。在内部，深度网络预测图像坐标中对象关键点（边界长方体的顶点）上的分布，然后在使用 PnP 计算最终姿势之前，采用新颖的概率过滤过程集成估计值。我们的框架允许系统在预测当前帧时考虑先前的不确定性，从而产生比单帧方法更准确和稳定的预测。大量实验表明，我们的方法在带注释的对象视频的具有挑战性的 Objectron 基准测试中优于现有方法。我们还展示了我们的工作在增强现实环境中的可用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于关键点的类别级物体6自由度位姿跟踪算法，通过RGB视频序列进行位姿估计并结合不确定性预测。
AI.motivation: 现有单帧方法在位姿估计中易受噪声影响，缺乏对前序信息和不确定性的有效利用，导致精度和稳定性不足。
AI.method: 采用单阶段网络预测物体关键点分布，结合前序帧信息与概率滤波过程，通过PnP算法计算最终位姿，同时量化预测不确定性。
AI.result: 在Objectron基准测试中优于现有方法，且在增强现实场景中验证了算法的实用性。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合时序信息与不确定性估计，该方法显著提升了类别级物体位姿跟踪的精度与鲁棒性。

title: Personalized Federated Learning with Server-Side Information
title_zh: 使用服务器端信息的个性化联合学习
abstract: Personalized Federated Learning (FL) is an emerging research field in FL that learns an easily adaptable global model in the presence of data heterogeneity among clients. However, one of the main challenges for personalized FL is the heavy reliance on clients' computing resources to calculate higher-order gradients since client data is segregated from the server to ensure privacy. To resolve this, we focus on a problem setting where the server may possess its own data independent of clients' data -- a prevalent problem setting in various applications, yet relatively unexplored in existing literature. Specifically, we propose FedSIM, a new method for personalized FL that actively utilizes such server data to improve meta-gradient calculation in the server for increased personalization performance. Experimentally, we demonstrate through various benchmarks and ablations that FedSIM is superior to existing methods in terms of accuracy, more computationally efficient by calculating the full meta-gradients in the server, and converges up to 34.2% faster.
summary: Personalized Federated Learning (FL) is an emerging research field in FL that learns an easily adaptable global model in the presence of data heterogeneity among clients. However, one of the main challenges for personalized FL is the heavy reliance on clients' computing resources to calculate higher-order gradients since client data is segregated from the server to ensure privacy. To resolve this, we focus on a problem setting where the server may possess its own data independent of clients' data -- a prevalent problem setting in various applications, yet relatively unexplored in existing literature. Specifically, we propose FedSIM, a new method for personalized FL that actively utilizes such server data to improve meta-gradient calculation in the server for increased personalization performance. Experimentally, we demonstrate through various benchmarks and ablations that FedSIM is superior to existing methods in terms of accuracy, more computationally efficient by calculating the full meta-gradients in the server, and converges up to 34.2% faster.
abstract_zh: 个性化联合学习（FL）是 FL 中的一个新兴研究领域，它在客户之间存在数据异构性的情况下学习易于适应的全局模型。然而，个性化 FL 的主要挑战之一是严重依赖客户端的计算资源来计算高阶梯度，因为客户端数据与服务器隔离以确保隐私。为了解决这个问题，我们关注一个问题设置，其中服务器可能拥有独立于客户端数据的自己的数据——这是各种应用程序中普遍存在的问题设置，但在现有文献中相对未经探索。具体来说，我们提出了 FedSIM，这是一种个性化 FL 的新方法，它积极利用此类服务​​器数据来改进服务器中的元梯度计算，以提高个性化性能。在实验上，我们通过各种基准测试和消融证明，FedSIM 在准确性方面优于现有方法，通过计算服务器中的完整元梯度，计算效率更高，并且收敛速度提高了 34.2%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedSIM方法，通过利用服务器端独立数据优化元梯度计算，解决个性化联邦学习中客户端计算负担重和隐私保护的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有个性化联邦学习过度依赖客户端计算高阶梯度，导致计算开销大且难以保障隐私，而服务器端通常存在独立数据这一特性尚未被充分挖掘。
AI.method: 设计FedSIM方法，通过服务器端独立数据辅助计算完整元梯度，减少客户端参与计算的负担，提升模型个性化性能。
AI.result: 实验表明FedSIM在多个基准测试中准确率优于现有方法，计算效率提升显著，收敛速度最快可达34.2%。
AI.conclusion: FedSIM通过有效利用服务器端数据，实现了更高效、更精准的个性化联邦学习，为未来联邦学习架构设计提供了新思路。

authors_text: Xueyuan Lin,Haihong E,Gengxian Zhou,Tianyi Hu,Li Ningyuan,Mingzhi Sun,Haoran Luo
title: FLEX: Feature-Logic Embedding Framework for CompleX Knowledge Graph Reasoning
title_zh: FLEX：用于复杂知识图推理的特征逻辑嵌入框架
abstract: Current best performing models for knowledge graph reasoning (KGR) introduce geometry objects or probabilistic distributions to embed entities and first-order logical (FOL) queries into low-dimensional vector spaces. They can be summarized as a center-size framework (point/box/cone, Beta/Gaussian distribution, etc.). However, they have limited logical reasoning ability. And it is difficult to generalize to various features, because the center and size are one-to-one constrained, unable to have multiple centers or sizes. To address these challenges, we instead propose a novel KGR framework named Feature-Logic Embedding framework, FLEX, which is the first KGR framework that can not only TRULY handle all FOL operations including conjunction, disjunction, negation and so on, but also support various feature spaces. Specifically, the logic part of feature-logic framework is based on vector logic, which naturally models all FOL operations. Experiments demonstrate that FLEX significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets.
summary: Current best performing models for knowledge graph reasoning (KGR) introduce geometry objects or probabilistic distributions to embed entities and first-order logical (FOL) queries into low-dimensional vector spaces. They can be summarized as a center-size framework (point/box/cone, Beta/Gaussian distribution, etc.). However, they have limited logical reasoning ability. And it is difficult to generalize to various features, because the center and size are one-to-one constrained, unable to have multiple centers or sizes. To address these challenges, we instead propose a novel KGR framework named Feature-Logic Embedding framework, FLEX, which is the first KGR framework that can not only TRULY handle all FOL operations including conjunction, disjunction, negation and so on, but also support various feature spaces. Specifically, the logic part of feature-logic framework is based on vector logic, which naturally models all FOL operations. Experiments demonstrate that FLEX significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets.
abstract_zh: 当前性能最佳的知识图推理 (KGR) 模型引入几何对象或概率分布，将实体和一阶逻辑 (FOL) 查询嵌入到低维向量空间中。它们可以概括为中心尺寸框架（点/盒/锥、Beta/高斯分布等）。然而，他们的逻辑推理能力有限。而且很难泛化到各种特征，因为中心和尺寸是一一约束的，无法有多个中心或尺寸。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的 KGR 框架，称为特征逻辑嵌入框架 FLEX，这是第一个 KGR 框架，不仅可以真正处理所有 FOL 操作，包括合取、析取、否定等，而且还支持各种特征空间。具体来说，特征逻辑框架的逻辑部分基于向量逻辑，它自然地对所有 FOL 操作进行建模。实验表明，FLEX 在基准数据集上的性能显着优于现有的最先进方法。

AI.tldr: 提出FLEX框架，解决知识图谱推理中逻辑处理能力有限和特征泛化不足的问题
AI.motivation: 现有方法基于中心-尺寸框架，难以处理多中心/多尺寸特征，导致逻辑推理能力受限且泛化性差
AI.method: 基于向量逻辑构建特征-逻辑嵌入框架，通过解耦特征空间与逻辑运算实现多中心/多尺寸建模
AI.result: 在基准数据集上显著优于现有最先进方法
AI.conclusion: FLEX通过向量逻辑与特征解耦设计，有效解决了知识图谱复杂推理的逻辑完备性与特征泛化性矛盾
